name: Applesaucy Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Light brown sugar, firmly packed
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
1 Chicken (3 1/2 to 4 pounds), cut into 8 pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Start fire in grill, placing rack 4 inches above coals.
2. Heat oil in a 1-quart saucepan over medium-high heat; add onion; cook about 5 minutes, stirring often, until softened.
3. Add applesauce, ketchup, vinegar, sugar, Worcestershire sauce, and pepper to the pan; bring to a boil, stirring.
4. Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until mixture is slightly thickened. Remove from heat and set aside.
5. Place chicken pieces on hot grill rack, skin side down. Cook covered with grill cover for 15 minutes.
6. Turn pieces over; cook, covered, another 10 minutes.
7. Brush applesauce mixture liberally over chicken; cook, covered, an additional 5 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
8. Serve garnished with herb sprigs.
NOTE: Chicken may be baked in the oven. Prepare applesauce mixture as directed. Heat oven to 400°F. Arrange chicken pieces, skin side up, in a baking or roasting pan; bake for 20 minutes. Brush with applesauce mixture; bake for an additional 10 minutes until golden brown and cooked through. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
